casestar-turbo.fact: CaseStar-Turbo: full-stack forensic intelligence platform for public defenders at ~/CaseStar-Turbo/

Core insight: Brain + semantic embeddings + local zombie AI = CaseStar document intelligence engine. Patient uploads medical records → extracted as structured facts → embedded → local LLM answers questions by citing actual documents. Private (HIPAA-safe), free, accurate. This is the bridge between rathin_utils Brain system and CaseStar product.

P0 FIX: embedding_status field added to Chunk dataclass ("real", "zero_fallback", "none"). Zero-vectors now filtered from vector search in rag_service.py. Magnitude check (norm < 1e-6) as safety net for legacy data. embed_with_status() returns (embedding, status) tuples.

P0 FIX: BM25 index now cached per HybridRAG instance via _build_bm25_index(). Uses rank_bm25.BM25Okapi (proper IDF scoring). Index built once, invalidated when chunk count changes. Eliminates O(N) full-corpus tokenization per query.

CaseStar private fork created 2026-02-27: github.com/KHET-1/CaseStar (private). Remote 'casestar' added to ~/CaseStar-Turbo/ repo alongside origin (CaseStar-Turbo). Both remotes synced on single 'main' branch. Original CaseStar-Turbo remains public and untouched.

Rescued services integration (2026-02-27): 47 files from 4 drives integrated. 9 Python services moved to backend/app/services/ (ocr_enhanced, intelligent_qa_cache, knowledge_base_builder, knowledge_rag_service, knowledge_worker, persona_loader, audioslooth_detector, transcription_service, diterm_service). Rust diterm → tools/diterm/. Docs → docs/rescued/{instructions,commanders}. rescued/ dir deleted.

POST /agent/route endpoint added to agent_routes.py (2026-02-27). Calls route_to_agent() from agent_router.py — auto-routes queries to specialized agents (MEDICAL/TIMELINE/CHAIN/GENERAL) via keyword detection. Supports Ollama and LM Studio backends with auto-detection.

v1.1 ingestion pipeline (post-1024d migration 2026-02-27): Upload→Parse(PyMuPDF/Tesseract/EasyOCR)→Chunk(token-based, 300-1000 tokens, content-aware 6 profiles)→Embed(LM Studio mxbai-embed-large-v1 1024d primary GPU, FastEmbed bge-large-en-v1.5 1024d CPU fallback)→Store(ChromaDB HNSW M=32 ef=200)→Search(Hybrid BM25+Vector RRF+MMR). Since LM Studio is now PRIMARY embed backend, this is critical — embedding_service.py sets _lmstudio_available=False on failure and falls through to FastEmbed. Always run lms load MODEL --gpu max before ingestion. Check with lms ps. Reload ~3s.

bluetooth_audio.fact: WirePlumber bluez5.enable-hw-volume=true causes silent A2DP transport on Devialet Phantom II - data flows in pw-top and btmon shows packets sent but no sound. Fix: set bluez5.enable-hw-volume=false in WirePlumber rules.

WirePlumber session.suspend-timeout-seconds must be 0 for Phantom II BT - default suspend behavior causes transport to silently fail on resume. Config file: ~/.config/wireplumber/wireplumber.conf.d/51-phantom-fixes.conf

WirePlumber 50-bluez-config.conf MUST use bluez5.codecs=[sbc] only for Phantom II. AAC causes spa.bluez5 config error -95, SBC-XQ crashes transport. Having AAC/SBC-XQ in codec list causes WirePlumber link activation failure even when falling back to SBC.

Linux kernel 6.15.5+ has L2CAP MTU negotiation regression (BlueZ #1422/#1448) - kernel sends SBC frames exceeding negotiated MTU, speaker receives but cannot decode. Symptoms: transport active, packets flowing, silence. Kernel 6.12.x unaffected.

After bluetoothctl remove+re-pair, first connect gets ACL with A2DP endpoints. Subsequent reconnects after WirePlumber restart fall back to LE-only (no A2DP). Fix: always remove+re-pair for fresh ACL link if LE-only detected via hcitool con.

Devialet Phantom II does NOT auto-switch to Bluetooth source if previously on WiFi (Spotify Connect/AirPlay). Must use Devialet app to select BT input or factory reset. Speaker accepts A2DP and receives data but routes output to last WiFi source.

Silent A2DP diagnostic checklist: 1) hcitool con - ACL not LE, 2) pactl list cards - a2dp-sink active, 3) pw-top - QUANT/RATE non-zero, 4) btmon - ACL packet count, 5) journalctl wireplumber - link failures/config errors, 6) dbus MediaTransport1 - State=active

PipeWire quantum 512 (10.7ms) causes SBC Bluetooth broken pipe errors on Devialet Phantom II. Minimum safe quantum is 1024 (21.3ms). DiamondForge uses pw-metadata clock.force-quantum at runtime and should NOT force below 1024 when BT sink is active.

After removing Bluetooth pairing and re-pairing Phantom II, BlueZ may show le-connection-abort-by-local errors for 30-60 seconds. Fix: power cycle both the BT adapter (bluetoothctl power off/on) AND the Phantom (unplug 10s). The Intel AX211 adapter needs extended cooldown after pairing changes.

pipewire.fact: Multiple PipeWire restarts in a session break Firefox audio completely - Firefox holds stale PipeWire socket connections, videos/audio refuse to play (stuck at 0). Ghost streams in pactl. Simple Firefox restart insufficient - full system reboot needed.

ollama.fact: Ollama 0.17.4 installed at /usr/local/bin/ollama with CUDA libs at /usr/local/lib/ollama/. Upgrade: download tar.zst from GitHub releases, extract, sudo cp binary + libs, restart service.

maintenance.fact: pip cache can grow to 4+ GB silently. Run pip3 cache purge periodically. Checked 2026-02-27: purged 4.7 GB.

neon_rush.fact: Asteroid breakup mechanic implemented (2026-02-28): Large(45HP,500pts)→2 Medium(30HP,250pts)→2 Small(15HP,100pts)→death. Full chain=11 bolts, 4 terminal kills, 1400pts. take_hit() returns dict {terminal:bool, fragments:[...]} — truthy like True but carries fragment spawn info. Only terminal kills count toward ASTEROID_CLEAR_TARGET(20). Nuke bypasses fragmentation. asteroid_split SFX added.

V2 headless road_geometry bug (2026-02-28): road_geometry.advance()+compute_projection() only called in bg.update_and_draw() which is inside draw(). Headless mode never calls draw() so V2 sprite projection stays stale. Fix: bg.tick_road(speed) method callable from update() with _rg_ticked flag to prevent double-advance when draw IS called.

desert_sprites.py init order bug (2026-02-28): Obstacle/Coin/PowerUp classes called self._draw() before self.tier and self.pulse were assigned. _draw() uses both. Fix: set self.tier and self.pulse BEFORE calling self._draw(). Same class of bug as the FONT_POWERUP import aliasing — attribute access before assignment.

Autoplay AI fixes (2026-02-28): 1) FakeKeys heuristic never fired heat bolts during asteroid phase (only boss_active). Added mode.phase=="asteroids" check. 2) V2 asteroids scatter across road width but bolts go straight up — AI must steer toward nearest asteroid x position. Without targeting, bolts miss all V2 road-projected asteroids. 3) Learning brain boost logic also needed asteroid phase awareness (save heat for bolts).

Autoplay test results post-asteroid-breakup (2026-02-28): Tier 1 — 6/6 VICTORY, avg 46k frames, 190k pts. Tier 2 (evo) — 5/6 runs beat 3/3 bosses and started cycling, 2 runs beat 4 bosses. Commit 7e65d98: 23 files, +2764/-380 lines. Includes V2 evolution tier, pseudo-3D road projection, and visual upgrades.

devialet_phantom_bredr_connection.fact: Devialet Phantom II 14:5A:FC:69:9B:F2 requires sync button hold to activate BR/EDR radio for A2DP. Without sync button, only BLE connects (no audio). Procedure: 1) remove device, 2) hold sync button, 3) bluetoothctl pair, 4) trust, 5) connect. ACL connection = success, LE connection = no audio. Set PreferredBearer=bredr in /var/lib/bluetooth/.../info after pairing.

pipewire_quantum_sbc_bluetooth.fact: PipeWire quantum 512 causes broken pipe on SBC Bluetooth. Quantum 1024 is the safe floor for Devialet Phantom II SBC codec. Config at ~/.config/pipewire/pipewire.conf.d/10-bluetooth-performance.conf. DiamondForge uses pw-metadata to force quantum=1024 at startup and reset to 0 on shutdown.

phantom_speaker_test_broken_pipe.fact: speaker-test with tiny period (-p 2) causes broken pipe on BT sinks. Use pw-play with proper WAV files for testing Bluetooth audio output. pw-play handles buffer negotiation correctly.

phantom_full_audio_pipeline_working.fact: Full audio pipeline verified 2026-03-01: PipeWire 1.4.2 -> bluez_output.14_5A_FC_69_9B_F2.1 -> SBC A2DP -> Devialet Phantom II 98dB. Connection: ACL BR/EDR (NOT BLE). Quantum 1024. Volume 100%. Key: sync button MUST be held during initial pairing to activate BR/EDR radio. After pairing, set PreferredBearer=bredr in /var/lib/bluetooth/.../info. Phantom may need time after connect before audio routes to amplifier (internal source switching delay).

devialet_phantom_ii_linux_full_pipeline.fact:
COMPLETE Devialet Phantom II Bluetooth Audio Pipeline on Linux (Parrot Security 7.0):

PROBLEM: Phantom II connects via BLE (Low Energy) by default. BLE provides GATT services but NOT A2DP audio. 
A2DP requires BR/EDR (Classic Bluetooth ACL connection). BlueZ defaults to BLE, so audio sink never appears in PipeWire.

ROOT CAUSE: Phantom II is dual-mode BT (BLE + BR/EDR). The BR/EDR radio is DORMANT by default.
Only activates when the physical SYNC BUTTON on the speaker is held down.

FIX PROCEDURE:
1. bluetoothctl remove 14:5A:FC:69:9B:F2 (remove old pairing)
2. User holds SYNC button on Phantom (LED pulses white)
3. bluetoothctl scan on (immediately)
4. bluetoothctl pair 14:5A:FC:69:9B:F2 (within seconds of scan finding it)
5. bluetoothctl trust 14:5A:FC:69:9B:F2
6. bluetoothctl connect 14:5A:FC:69:9B:F2
7. Verify: hcitool con shows ACL (not LE) connection type
8. Verify: D-Bus shows sep1-4 endpoints + transport fd0
9. Verify: pw-cli shows bluez_output.14_5A_FC_69_9B_F2.1

POST-PAIR LOCK (prevent BLE reconnection):
Edit /var/lib/bluetooth/{adapter}/{device}/info:
  Set PreferredBearer=bredr (was last-seen)
  Verify both LinkKey (BR/EDR) and LongTermKey (BLE) sections exist

PIPEWIRE CONFIG:
~/.config/pipewire/pipewire.conf.d/10-bluetooth-performance.conf:
  default.clock.quantum = 1024 (safe floor for SBC codec)
  default.clock.min-quantum = 256
  default.clock.max-quantum = 8192

CODEC: SBC joint stereo, 48kHz, bitpool 88
PLAYBACK: Use pw-play (NOT speaker-test, which causes broken pipe with small periods)
VOLUME: wpctl set-volume @DEFAULT_AUDIO_SINK@ 1.0

WARNING: Restarting WirePlumber drops the BT connection and reconnection defaults to BLE.
WARNING: btmon requires sudo/root to capture HCI packets (0 packets without privileges is access issue, not data issue).

wireplumber_phantom_ii_configs.fact:
WirePlumber configs for Devialet Phantom II:

50-bluez-config.conf: SBC-only codec, no hw-volume, no suspend timeout
51-phantom-fixes.conf: Phantom-specific WirePlumber fixes  
52-diamondforge-priority.conf: DiamondForge priority 1250, Firefox demoted to 800

Location: ~/.config/wireplumber/wireplumber.conf.d/
CRITICAL: Back these up before any nuke-and-pave. Restoring from .bak after WP restart works.
DO NOT restart WirePlumber while Phantom is connected - it drops the BT connection.

bluetooth_ble_vs_bredr_audio.fact:
BLE vs BR/EDR for Bluetooth Audio:
- BLE (Low Energy): GATT services only. NO audio streaming capability. Used for control/metadata.
- BR/EDR (Classic): Required for A2DP audio streaming. Uses ACL connection type.
- Dual-mode devices may connect via BLE first, leaving A2DP unavailable.
- hcitool con shows connection type: "< LE" = BLE only, "< ACL" = BR/EDR (good for audio)
- ConnectProfile for A2DP returns br-connection-page-timeout when device BR/EDR radio is dormant.
- D-Bus path org.bluez /dev_XX_XX shows sep/endpoint nodes ONLY on BR/EDR connection.
- PreferredBearer in /var/lib/bluetooth/.../info controls reconnection preference.

network_recon.name: Full Network Enumeration
network_recon.prompt: Perform comprehensive network reconnaissance on [TARGET/RANGE]. Phase 1: Host discovery (ping sweep, ARP scan). Phase 2: Port scan top 1000 then full 65535 on live hosts. Phase 3: Service version detection + OS fingerprint on open ports. Phase 4: Run relevant NSE scripts per service. Present results as a structured table: IP | Open Ports | Services | OS | Notable Findings. Flag anything unusual. Save scan results to ~/recon/[target]-[date]/. Suggest attack surface priorities ranked by risk.
network_recon.reason: Comprehensive 4-phase methodology. Forces structured output, saves results, and prioritizes findings. Covers the full recon lifecycle in one prompt.

network_recon.name: Quick Tactical Scan
network_recon.prompt: Fast recon on [TARGET]. Nmap top 100 ports with service detection (-sV -sC --top-ports 100 -T4). Parse output into a clean table. For each service found, list: known default creds, common CVEs for that version, and suggested next tool to use. Keep it tight — I need actionable intel in under 2 minutes.
network_recon.reason: Speed-optimized. Gets actionable intel fast with CVE and default cred awareness built in. Perfect for time-boxed engagements.

web_security.name: Full Web App Assessment
web_security.prompt: Assess the web application at [URL]. Workflow: 1) Tech stack fingerprint. 2) Directory bruteforce with ffuf/gobuster. 3) Check for: SQLi entry points, XSS reflection, IDOR patterns, auth bypass, directory traversal, exposed admin panels, sensitive files (.git, .env, robots.txt). 4) Check HTTP security headers (CORS, CSP, X-Frame, HSTS). Present findings severity-ranked: Finding | Severity | Evidence | Remediation.
web_security.reason: Full OWASP coverage in a single prompt. Forces evidence-based findings with remediation. The severity ranking makes triage immediate.

web_security.name: Targeted Endpoint Test
web_security.prompt: Test [URL/ENDPOINT] for [VULN_TYPE]. Show exact payloads, explain why each works against common filters, execute them, capture responses, determine vulnerability. If vulnerable: demonstrate impact. If not: explain defenses and suggest bypasses. Document everything reproducibly.
web_security.reason: Surgical precision testing. The 'explain why' and 'suggest bypasses' make it a learning tool AND a testing tool simultaneously.

python_dev.name: Build a Pro Tool
python_dev.prompt: Build a Python tool that [DESCRIPTION]. Requirements: argparse CLI, proper error handling, colored terminal output (colorama/rich), modular functions, --verbose flag, --output flag, banner header. Make it professional — this goes in my toolkit. Test before delivering. If network-touching, add timeout and rate limiting.
python_dev.reason: Forces production-quality code every time. The 'goes in my toolkit' framing ensures it's reusable, not throwaway. Built-in quality gates.

python_dev.name: Automate a Workflow
python_dev.prompt: Automate [WORKFLOW]. Python. Chain steps logically. Progress indicators (tqdm/rich). Handle failures gracefully — log and continue. Structured output (JSON/CSV). Summary: total processed, successes, failures, elapsed time. Make it idempotent — safe to re-run.
python_dev.reason: The idempotent requirement is key — prevents double-processing disasters. Progress indicators keep long jobs visible. Failure handling means it doesn't die on edge cases.

osint.name: Full OSINT Profile
osint.prompt: Gather OSINT on [TARGET]. Passive only. Check: WHOIS, DNS (A/MX/TXT/NS/CNAME), subdomains, SSL cert transparency, web archive, tech stack, social media, email patterns, exposed docs/metadata, GitHub/GitLab, paste sites. Compile intelligence report. Rate confidence (high/medium/low) per finding. Suggest deeper investigation targets.
osint.reason: Exhaustive passive checklist ensures nothing is missed. Confidence ratings prevent acting on noise. The 'suggest deeper' at the end chains into the next action naturally.

osint.name: Quick Footprint
osint.prompt: Fast passive footprint of [TARGET]. DNS + WHOIS + subdomain enum + tech stack + exposed services. 5-minute time box. Bullet points. Flag top 3 most interesting findings first. Suggest single best next action.
osint.reason: Time-boxed to prevent rabbit holes. The 'top 3 first' ensures the most valuable intel is front and center even if you stop reading.

exploit_dev.name: CVE Research & PoC
exploit_dev.prompt: Research [CVE/VULNERABILITY]. Find: affected versions, root cause, attack vector, prerequisites, existing PoCs. Review public PoC code for safety, adapt for our environment. If no PoC, analyze details and draft one. Test controlled. Document: vulnerable config, exploitation steps, detection indicators, patch/mitigation.
exploit_dev.reason: Full CVE lifecycle from research to PoC to documentation. The 'detection indicators' make it useful for both offense and defense.

exploit_dev.name: Security Code Review
exploit_dev.prompt: Analyze [BINARY/SOURCE] for security issues. Binaries: checksec, identify functions, trace input handling, memory corruption. Source: injection points, auth flaws, logic bugs, race conditions, crypto misuse. Rank by exploitability. Top finding: outline exploitation path.
exploit_dev.reason: Dual binary/source methodology. The 'rank by exploitability' prevents wasting time on theoretical issues. Exploitation path proves impact.

reporting.name: Full Assessment Report
reporting.prompt: Compile findings into professional report. Structure: Executive Summary (3 sentences), Scope, Methodology, Findings (severity-ranked table with CVSS-style rating), Detailed Findings (description, evidence, impact, remediation each), Conclusion & Recommendations. Markdown. Include command outputs as evidence. Client-ready.
reporting.reason: Client-ready output structure. The 3-sentence exec summary forces clarity. CVSS-style rating standardizes severity. Evidence requirement prevents fluff.

reporting.name: Tactical Briefing
reporting.prompt: Summarize [RESULTS] as tactical briefing. BLUF (1-2 sentences), Key Findings (numbered), Risk Assessment (critical/high/medium/low counts), Recommended Actions (prioritized), Open Questions. One screen of terminal output. No fluff.
reporting.reason: BLUF format is military-grade communication. The 'one screen' constraint forces brutal prioritization. Open Questions keeps transparency.

maintenance.name: ## Dragonfly Stack Upgrade Check Run...
maintenance.prompt:
## Dragonfly Stack Upgrade Check
Run at session start or when user asks. Check all 13 tools:

**Binary tools** (GitHub releases → ~/.local/bin):
rg (BurntSushi/ripgrep), gh (cli/cli), fzf (junegunn/fzf), bat (sharkdp/bat),
fd (sharkdp/fd), jq (jqlang/jq), yq (mikefarah/yq), eza (eza-community/eza),
zoxide (ajeetdsouza/zoxide), atuin (atuinsh/atuin)

**Pip tools** (pip3 install --upgrade --break-system-packages):
httpie, thefuck, tldr

**Quick version check pattern:**
- Installed: run `tool --version` (eza: line 2)
- Latest: `curl -sI https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest | grep -i ^location | awk -F/ '{print $NF}'`
- Pip: `pip3 index versions PACKAGE | head -1`

**Upgrade pattern (binaries):**
curl -sL RELEASE_URL -o /tmp/tool.tar.gz → extract → cp to ~/.local/bin/

Present results as table: Tool | Installed | Latest | Status
Only upgrade what user approves.
maintenance.reason: Reusable workflow for checking and upgrading all Dragonfly CLI tools — avoids re-discovering repos and version commands each session

scouts.context: Deep intel on Metasploit Framework — advanced module types, msfvenom payload generation patterns, post-exploitation modules, pivoting techniques, session management. Focus on patterns not in basic tutorials.

findings.fact: Staged payloads (windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp with slash) send a small 73KB stager that establishes TCP, reads 4 bytes for payload size, allocates RWX memory, and downloads the full stage. Stageless payloads (windows/meterpreter_reverse_tcp with underscore) bundle everything in ~245KB. Staged evades more AV since few vendors beacon for Stage 2.

post/multi/manage/autoroute 'autoadd' CMD auto-discovers valid subnets from session routing table and interfaces. 'default' CMD adds catch-all route forcing all unspecified TCP/IP through the pivot. Netmask accepts both dotted-decimal (255.255.255.0) and CIDR (/24) notation.

'sessions -u' upgrade is limited to default reverse Meterpreter and CANNOT work through pivots. For pivoting, use full post/multi/manage/shell_to_meterpreter with manually set LHOST pointing to pivot machine IP, as session object shows 'Local Pipe' instead of correct address.

makerc command records all msfconsole commands into .rc resource script, eliminating typing errors. Resource scripts support ERB (Embedded Ruby) for calling APIs, interacting with DB objects, and programmatic command generation. Run with 'resource script.rc' or 'msfconsole -r script.rc'.

Workspaces isolate data per engagement: 'workspace -a name' creates, 'workspace name' switches. Each workspace scopes all DB queries (hosts, services, vulns). Combined with 'db_export -f xml backup.xml' for pre-reinit backup. Route add accepts session IDs as gateway: 'route add 10.0.0.0 255.255.0.0 <session_id>'.

scouts.context: Network reconnaissance techniques beyond basic nmap — passive OS fingerprinting, ARP tricks, IPv6 discovery, DNS zone transfers, SNMP enumeration, service-specific probing patterns. Practical field knowledge.

findings.fact: p0f performs passive OS fingerprinting by analyzing TCP/IP stack characteristics (window size, initial TTL, TCP options sequence) from SYN/SYN+ACK without sending probes. Common TTL defaults: 64 (Linux), 128 (Windows), 255 (Cisco/network gear). TCP window size alone gives 70-80% OS detection accuracy.

IPv6 host discovery via multicast: ping6 -I <iface> ff02::1 (all-nodes) and ff02::2 (all-routers) on link-local scope. Responses populate neighbor cache (IPv6 ARP equivalent). View with 'ip -6 neigh'. Bypasses firewalls that only filter IPv4 discovery.

RFC 9276 (2022) recommends NSEC3 iterations=0 and empty salt — additional iterations provide minimal security against zone enumeration since GPUs crack most NSEC3 hashes. Modern attackers use rainbow tables to enumerate NSEC3-protected zones despite hashing.

SNMPv2c introduced 64-bit counters (ifHCInOctets OID 1.3.6.1.2.1.31.1.1.1.6) to prevent wrap on high-speed interfaces. At 1Gbps, 32-bit counters wrap in 34 seconds making SNMPv1 useless for gigabit+ monitoring. Always use v2c/v3 for accurate traffic data.

SMB null session enumeration (rpcclient -U '' -N <target>) extracts users, shares, groups, SIDs without auth. Default on Win2000/NT, disabled since XP SP2/Server 2003. Still common on legacy/misconfigured Samba. Key commands: enumdomusers, querydispinfo, netshareenumall.

scouts.context: Advanced ffuf techniques — filter chains (-fc/-fs/-fw/-fl), recursive fuzzing, multi-wordlist mode, rate limiting evasion, custom header injection, POST data fuzzing, virtual host discovery, calibration mode. Real operator patterns.

findings.fact: Autocalibrate mode (-ac) auto-filters noise; -mc all -ac matches all codes while filtering
Multi-wordlist: -w list:KEYWORD, modes: clusterbomb (default), pitchfork, sniper
POST fuzzing: -X POST -d with FUZZ in body, -H for headers, supports JSON
Vhost discovery: fuzz Host header -H 'Host: FUZZ.target' -fs to filter default
Recursion: -recursion -recursion-depth N, strategies: default (30x) vs greedy (all)
